name: Chocolate Chip & Almond Biscotti
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F and adjust racks to create three thirds.
Use cookie sheets with two flat edges, or flip them upside down.
2. Line the sheets with parchment paper.
3. In a large bowl, sift together flour, baking soda, baking powder, and salt.
4. Add sugar and stir until mixed.
5. Process about 1/2 cup of dry ingredients in a food processor with
about 1/2 cup toasted almonds for about 30 seconds, or until the nuts are fine and powdery.
6. Add the processed mixture to the sifted ingredients and remaining toasted almonds and chocolate chips; stir until mixed.
7. In a small bowl, beat eggs, vanilla, and whiskey with a whisk until just combined.
8. Add egg mixture to dry ingredients and hand-stir until dry ingredients are moistened.
9. Place dough on parchment next to the sink, wetting your hands with cold water.
10. Press the dough into a round mound on the parchment.
11. Cut the dough into equal quarters using a long heavy knife.
12. Form each piece of dough into strips measuring 9 inches long,
2-2.5 inches wide, and about 1/2 inch high. Do not roll; press gently.
13. Place two strips crosswise on each lined sheet.
14. Bake for 25 minutes, then reverse sheets top to bottom and front to back once during baking.
15. Remove sheets from oven and slide parchment off.
16. Transfer baked strips to a large cutting board and let cool for 20 minutes.
17. Reduce oven temperature to 275°F.
18. Cut the cooled strips into slices about 1/2 inch wide using a serrated bread knife, sawing motion.
19. Place slices cut-side down on unlined sheets.
20. Bake two sheets with reversed slices, turning once during baking and reversing top to bottom and front to back.
21. Bake for an additional 25-30 minutes until firm.
22. Turn off the oven, open the door, and let biscotti cool in the oven before storing in an airtight container.
